item.title: 140 kg (48% fat) - 86 kg (12-13% fat)
item.description: 6-7 years in the making. Turning 49 on March 31st. Never ever give up! P.S I do use Snapchat if people want to see my fitness journey. Username is ace1973 or Instagram: robertoficeland :) Thank for all the comments. It's nice to see a decent community who supports one another! :)
item.dc:creator: bobbyperu1973

item.title: Every day I wake up and hope this man is still alive...
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy visited wounded Ukrainian soldiers at a hospital and gave them medals for their courage in the fight against Russia.

item.title: There is a hard time ahead of us, so we decided to do it now
item.description: Couples of Ukrainian servicemen got married in Kyiv region. Years later, they will tell the children that instead of wedding bells, they heard artillery shelling. This is an indicator that Ukrainians, even in battle, remain people who love, pity and dream. It will be recalled that according to the laws of war, military chaplains and commanders of military units have the right to combine their destinies. If there is a person you love for a long time, do not hesitate.
item.dc:creator: xQuatiqXraqx

item.title: Dolly Parton Removes Herself from R&RHOF List
item.description:
Another example of what a class act she is. Really should just change it to the Music Hall of Fame. Especially if they're going to include so many non-rock genres.
From her Instagram

item.title: Day 290: of cheeseburgers I want inside of me
item.description: Mac Rib Double Smash Beef Patty, American Cheese, Smoked Brisket Sautéed In House BBQ Sauce, Slow-Smoked Beef Rib, Southern-style Comeback Sauce And Tangy Barbecue Sauce On 3 Pieces Of Texas Toast From Craft By Smoke and Fire/Smoke & Fire Social Eatery In Anaheim, La Habra, Paramount, Pomona, CA Thank You @jnco For This Burger Submission! 12/10 Previous Day 289: https://imgur.com/gallery/3Y1gwIA MESSAGE ME BURGER PICS OR YOUR FAVORITE BURGER SPOTS! Map of all burger spots that are available on yelp! Big thanks to @OhBoyDoIHaveNews4U for this. Link to his post and map https://imgur.com/gallery/vcObDuD

item.title: 21-year-old Ukrainian mathematician who represented Ukraine at the European Girls' Mathematical Olympiad and won a silver medal, was killed on March 8th, 2022 during a Russian attack on Kharkiv.

item.title: This is why we can't have nice things: Republicans.
item.dc:creator: ShySapiosexual

item.title: Pst, Hey, You! Wanna Learn About A Weapons-Grade Scumbag?
item.description:
Remember this guy? He probably doesn't look as familiar without his hair dye and in a prison jumpsuit, but fuck him anyhow. This is Paul Manafort, whom most of y'all probably first encountered when Robert Mueller's special counsel investigation arrested him for, oh gosh, a bunch of things, from "conspiracy against the U.S." [a charge that's not nearly as Old School Treason as it sounds], to money laundering, failing to register under FARA (more on that below), and a bunch of other "you were running a lot of shit off the radar, you scummy little weasel." So why the hell should you care? The "Russia investigation" never produced anything, Trump pardoned him in 2020 (because lol, the criminal justice system doesn't apply if you and your friends are rich and brazen enough, duh), and there's like...a dozen other crises going on, right? Thing is, this guy has been a World-Class, Weapons-Grade Fuckstick-for-Hire for decades, has made the world a measurably worse place, AND there's a really solid argument to be made that the situation in Ukraine may not be where it is if it were not for this guy's DIRECT ACTIONS. In fact, his own [adult] kids have said *he has a high body count* as the result of his fucking-about. If you want to skip to the Ukraine-specific stuff, drop down to the election maps of Ukraine! If you want more detail and you think my writing style isn't going to give you an aneurysm, then scroll below, and you're more optimistic than me, respectively.
Okay, so a quick primer here, and a disclaimer, not in that order because I am a disorganized motherfucker, bite me: 1) I'm not one of those Resistance types who tweets "BOOM:" or "GOTCHA:" and then some inane shit about how "The Feds Are Totally Gonna Arrest Trump This Time Because He Missed a Filing Deadline on his Taxes, Justice Wins Again, Follow and RT for More Justice News!" because I mean...c'mon. We've all gotten a crash course in how the system works and doesn't work and anyone else trying to sell you that kind of line in *2022* is working a grift or an angle. I write about this not because I want to screech "SEE HILLARY WAS RIGHT HASHTAG IM STILL WITH HER" on cable news, I write about it because Paul Manafort should've died in prison and he should be extradited to Ukraine as soon as they win [slava ukraini!]. Or even before then, I dunno, they can probably range their follow-up artillery shots if they launch him out of a circus cannon at a BMP-3. Anyway. 2) the Foreign Agents Registration Act was introduced after the federal gov't saw Americans (well, one or two in particular, but that's another post) running cover for Nazi Germany in the US in the 1930s; tamping down accusations of anti-Semitism, trying to make Nazi Germany look more modern and fascism/Nazism more acceptable, etc. Basically, if you're going to lobby for a foreign government/state's interests, you have to file paperwork saying it outright and disclose who's paying you, etc. (As always, the law and case law around it is more nuanced, but that's the gist.) This actually tamped down on lobbying by foreign governments for decades--the flak you'd catch as a...well, a PR flak for some random asshole overseas was not worth the money, more or less. Until!
A little lobbying firm named Black, Manafort, and Stone* thought "wait, what if we just lobby on behalf of the WORST GODDAMNED MONSTERS and charged them $buttloads for it?" See, Black, Manafort, and Stone had been one of Ronald Reagan's earliest campaign advisory groups. And they managed to do all kinds of wonderful underhanded things to help Reagan along through the primaries and into the White House by 1980. Because politics is a dirty game and it's only illegal if you get caught! And even then most of the time it's legal, because everyone wants to keep the gravy train coming and the dirty tricks in THEIR back pocket for when THEY can ratfuck someone. The great part about having plunked the Gipper in the White House? You've now got the ear of ol' Ronnie when it comes to getting your friends nice, cushy jobs. POP QUIZ, HOTSHOT: what did Ronald Reagan hate more than anything else? Okay, okay, not all at once, I'm hearing "the LGBTQ community," "unions," "the working class," "Congressional oversight," "being able to remember anything," "anyone not lily-white," "actually going overseas and or seeing combat in WWII**," "the idea of a social safety net or caring for your neighbor at all," wow, all great and correct answers, you're all well-read. But you forgot the one thing he hated the most: COMMUNISM. And he hated it so-gosh-darned-diggety-danged-much that we would just shovel guns and cash at ANYONE who said "we're fighting the dirty commies!" anywhere in the world! No matter what atrocities they committed in the meantime! Iran-Contra should've resulted in his impeachment and imprisonment along with HW Bush, but hey, law talks, money walks, or something. *YEP, *that* Roger Stone, both because truly great finks of history run in the same circles forever, always, time is a flat circle and there's only so many main named characters politics is apparently allowed to have at once. **to be fair, apparently his eyesight disqualified him, but real telling how folks who avoided combat have historically been some of the most gung-ho about getting a buncha folks killed in war
So say you're Paul Manafort, and you're an amoral dickbag. [But I repeat myself.] What's a REALLY GOOD gig to get? That's right, class! "Representing terrible dictatorships the world over and getting them access to American money and guns by telling the administration they're 'anti-communist'!" You hear those screams of massacred dissidents, little Timmy? That's just the bells of freedom! Pic: case in point, Ferdinand Marcos, dictator of the Philippines from 1965-1986, responsible for over 3,200 extrajudicial murders, the torture of ten times that many, and the forced disappearance/arrest of over 70,000. A perfect example of one of many of Manafort's clients! (Manafort struck out on his own/with another firm from Black, Manafort and Stone in the 90s.)
To those who skipped here from the intro: Welcome! We missed you! To those who read the whole way through: Good lord, what is WRONG with you? Seek some help. I've got enough time to write this but no-one should have enough time to read it all, goddamn. At any rate, welcome to the (final run off) presidential election of 2004 in Ukraine! You'll notice that Yushchenko (a candidate who leaned more pro-EU) won, fairly handily. Which is pretty impressive considering he was POISONED* a couple months before the election with dioxin [which is a real thing, despite the "Michael Bay coming up with a poison" name], leaving him deathly ill. The other candidate, Yanukovych, was a staunchly pro-Russian Euroskeptic who threatened to withdraw the country from a number of extant and pending economic agreements with the EU that would've brought Ukraine closer to the common economic sphere. Oh, and Yanukovych 'won' the first run-off, with astoundingly increased rates of turnout in certain districts and other irregularities that was definitely the result of ballot-stuffing, intimidation, and other fraud. He had been down double-digits in the polls and then 'won' by several points. After the entire international community except Russia went "hang on a tic" and the Ukrainian people went "hmm" to the tune of "massive public demonstrations that came to be called the Orange Revolution," the Ukrainian Supreme Court ruled the results invalid and ordered a re-run-off, with the results above. *good thing we don't have open-source journalism that's tracked Russian FSB assassins across Europe, retroactively ID'ing them over a decade later, closely linked to the death of Russian political dissidents, journalists, and other 'problems', in EXACTLY this same style or anything. Hang on, I'm being passed a note here...oh yeah, Bellingcat did exactly that. Check 'em out sometime.
Yanukovych [pictured here with some random dude for no reason whatsoever] was pissed and ironically screamed about election fraud, but stepped aside to allow Yushchenko to take office. And immediately began to plot his moves for the next election. Which is when a Russian metals oligarch (Oleg Deripashka, who pops up in the Muller investigation years later because, again, only so many main characters allowed here) sent him to Ukraine to lobby for his interests and Manafort ended up making contact with the Yanukovych campaign, being hired as an advisor. Manafort's advice? Bring some of that good ol' fashioned American Culture War and exploit the SHIT out of any divide you could, just pick some small fracture and hammer on it for weeks on end to polarize regions as much as you could. Go riiiiiight up to the edge of incitement and build a furious base through whatever bullshit-du-jour you can come up with. And as anyone who's paid attention to American politics since, uh....1980...1968...all of the Reconstruction-era...1820...literally ever...can tell you: that shit WORKS Assuming "works" means "gets you elected but at terrible cost to your country and the body politic with potentially violent or dangerous ramifications down the road." But hey, you don't have to worry about the little people--you're in POWER, now!
Yanukovych (blue, here) won! Narrowly, but he did win in the 2010 elections and observers affirmed it was a reasonably above-board election and a legitimate victory. So You've Just Culture War'd Yourself Into A Presidency: a guide: 0) well obviously you've already immediately imprisoned your political rival, not even a step, c'mon now, this ain't amateur hour 1) immediately appoint your friends to everything 2) begin construction of a giant dacha/palace/fun digs to chill at 3) more or less loot as much as you can 4) turn the national police into a personal secret police force and if you're Paul Manafort? Well remember how his whole schtick in the Reagan years was "I sell access"? This is now that in a country that doesn't have the *relatively* rigid controls as the US. Go hog wild! Sell access to everything to everyone! Let every oligarch come in and plunder and commit human rights abuses with workers to their weaselly black hearts' content! And pocket as much as you can--and this part is VERY IMPORTANT, TAKE NOTES--without telling the US federal government a single thing about a single cent. Also own a ton of real estate, leverage yourself to the balls in debt, get an expensive mistress that'll eventually out you by posting on social media about it, just go hog wild. Maybe ostrich leather jackets. Sky's the limit, hoss!
[Pictured here: the Euromaidan protests. Euro as in "Eurozone/European Union" and Maidan for the Maidan Nezalezhnosti, Independence Square in Kyiv.] Except, of course, when you've spent a decade fanning the flames of resentment and your administration is sketchy, there's only so long things can go before something goes wrong. In Yanukovych's case, it was his administration's sudden 2013 rejection of a trade agreement with the European Union that had passed through parliament with overwhelming support and was widely expected to be a shot in the arm to Ukraine's still-occasionally-struggling economy, bringing it closer to the EU and letting it access some of the EU markets more easily. Yanukovych, and by which I really mean Russia, was concerned about Ukraine slipping away from under Russian domination, and Yanukovych's sudden veto of the popular measure is about as good evidence as you can get that he saw his role as promoting Putin's interests first and the Ukrainian peoples' a distant second. Like a wwaaaaaay distant second. Probably fifth, really. Now, if you didn't know before this year you probably do now: the Ukrainian people ain't nothin' to fuck wit'.
[Protesters lighting a fire to obscure sniper sightlines, per Wikipedia] The protests that emerged after this decision in November 2013 made the Orange Revolution look like a slow farmers market; and here again Paul Manafort's oily hair breaks the surface in the narrative like a shark fin, except instead of a swimmer he's going after "protesters". Manafort was very much advising the government at the time, and by all reports was either a very loud voice for or The Voice For using violence on the protesters. See, the police reacted a bit, shall we say, harshly, with the peaceful protesters--gassing, beating, summary arrest, all kinds of things that would DEFINITELY NEVER HAPPEN IN THE US. So the next protesters were joined by MORE protesters, including, often, the parents and families of the beaten student protesters from before. Again, it really cannot be overstated: don't fuck with Ukrainians. They really, really don't like strongmen. This only escalated over the next few months, with running street battles between gov't forces and protesters, as well as protests in other cities throughout Ukraine. Protesters begun to occupy government buildings and erecting barricades [again, I recommend looking up on-the-ground reporting on this time from Robert Evans and other journalists, I cannot ever hope to do the story justice. Here's a random group of words to help: "ice fortress" "molotov cocktails" "tanks" "snipers" "possible Russian or Ukrainian secret-police provocateurs advised by FSB agents taking sniper shots at foot police to give cause for more force"] and things got violent. Like "snipers targetting protesters" to "open street battles" to "secret police opening fire on crowds and journalists with sidearms and shotguns" By Feb 20th, tens of THOUSANDS of protesters had arrived in Kyiv from all over the country, the death toll was in the dozens, civil order was breaking down, police departments from other regions were joining the protesters, the secret police were firing into the crowds with AK-pattern rifles, and it was pretty clear Shit Wasn't Going To End Well for the administration.
In the time-honored tradition of strongmen everywhere, Yanukovych and his administration booked it in the dead of night for Russia, within a day or two after signing an agreement to hand over power and call for snap elections. This may have had something to do with a bunch of uncomfortable questions like "isn't ordering your secret police to fire into unarmed crowds mass murder" and "i think that's a crime, you're probably gonna die in jail forever if you're lucky." Ukraine officials also wanted to talk to Manafort about his role in all this but oops--all gone! Back in the US! Who knows what he did or didn't do? Well, his daughters' texts were compromised and leaked, and in addition to a lot of...lurid and horrid allegations I will not repeat here for a number of reasons (warning: they do involve seriously heinous accounts of s*xual/spousal abuse of his wife and some truly awful stuff), there's a very...open acknowledgement of how awful their father is:
Of course, anyone who knows what's up in Ukraine now knows what happened next: the collapse of the Yanukovych regime and the regional tensions that Manafort had directly flamed for years to get Yanukovych elected gave Russia the fig leaf it needed to send its "Little Green Men" into Crimea--unmarked Russian special forces soldiers* that occupied the valuable region, cutting off or taking control of infrastructure and communications, claiming to be "concerned citizens", and de facto seizing Ukrainian land for Russia. A "referendum" on becoming part of Russia returned something like a very-believable 97.5% in favor. Which, to be fair, if you've got Russian Spetsnaz over your shoulder, you might vote in favor too. But they also just outright cheated other ways, too. This sparked the Russian-backed-separatist crisis that led to the regional conflict that has defined Ukraine from 2014-2022, which is the reason Ukraine's military is as ready as they were--this was an inevitable occasion. The war never ended. It just escalated. Meanwhile, Paul Manafort's lavish lifestyle and habit of not reporting any of it to the feds caught up with him when he was arrested for the charges mentioned at the beginning; he pled guilty to a number of them, took a cooperation plea, then CONTINUED to lie to investigators, got his plea deal tossed, was looking at even worse time, and then got pardoned by Trump in late 2020. Lol. And there's a serious case to be made the Euromaidan revolution would never have happened if Paul Manafort hadn't used his American politics playbook to get Yanukovych elected. AND that his encouraging of sectarian fractures and exploiting them for political gain gave power and grew separatist groups far beyond their limited scope--they may have died out or remained a sidelined group for years to come if Manafort hadn't openly steered Yanukovych towards reaching out to them. So where's Manafort's body count fall? The ~120 killed in the Euromaidan protests? The tens of thousands killed in the 2014-present Ukrainian war? Probably somewhere in between. But what about the body count of all the other evil bastards he represented? Who's to say. Regardless of what the number is: if there is a hell, Paul Manafort is destined for the furthest reaches. *Unidentified soldiers in this way is like, one of the oldest war crimes you can commit and one of the most clearly defined, and this was one of the most BLATANT instances of that ever, but hey, if we didn't hold Russia responsible for war crimes in 2000...or 2008...or Syria for a decade...or...hmm.
So, acknowledgements, citations, and "for further reading": First, if you read this far, you're as crazy as I am, and I like you but you scare me just a smidge. A wee bit. That said, I have not NEARLY scratched 1% of the surface of all of this, ESPECIALLY the Euromaidan revolution, and I highly encourage folks to go out and read more to better understand current events and also why holding folks accountable for violations of things like FARA are often WAY more important than they look on the surface. Imagine if he'd been arrested for his FARA violations in the mid-90s? Second, major credit to the podcast Behind the Bastards, hosted by Robert Evans, for a lot of the information here, and for turning me onto the idea of how THOROUGHLY a shitheel Paul Manafort was. The podcast is fantastic, Robert Evans is an excellent reporter, check it out if you want to be Mad About History Things. And hear a lot of jokes about Raytheon. Third, credit to the Mueller Report, because I do actually own a copy and read about 60% of it, because though the post-2016 stuff didn't really feature here a lot of the details about previous events came into sharper focus when reading the report. For example, Manafort worked as the Trump 2016 campaign manager for free. Why? Well the knee-jerk "everything is a Russian op" answer is "because he's a Russian agent", but that kind of oversimplification 1) makes Russia much scarier as a monolith than it is, and 2) ignores the facts on the ground. Manafort was deeply in debt, as the report details, and as both (I believe) report and the Behind the Bastards episodes surmise, Manafort was looking to sell access the way he did the Reagan admin if he got Trump into the White House. Oh, and Trump was one of Black, Manafort, and Stone's first ever clients way back when. Because of course he was. Fourth, credit to, well, a billion things I read when I had to write on some of this for other things a couple years ago. That's vague but I'm too lazy to track down the sourcing on a bunch of stuff for a law school paper from eons ago. Fifth, credit to Wikipedia for 1) helping me keep timelines straight, 2) spelling Yanukovych right, and 3) giving me a bunch of citations/footnotes to jump off of to read up on details. Sixth, I don't know, credit to my latent ADHD for giving me enough focus to write this all out and find pictures to break up the Wall of Text Slava Ukraini! Heroiam slava! Glory to Ukraine! Glory to the heroes!
item.dc:creator: DukePaulAtreides

item.title: I've curated some easy-to-read Ukraine updates for people that want to be informed but don't have time.
item.description:
Putin arrests the head of the FSB's foreign intelligence branch. Subsequently, many other high-ranking members of the FSB have been arrested. Reasons include embezzlement of funds, and sycophantically preaching that Ukraine was weak or would welcome the Russian orc "liberators" prior to starting their invasion, and in some cases for speaking out against the Russian invasion (or at least saying before the invasion that it wouldn't go well). https://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-10603045/Putin-places-head-FSBs-foreign-intelligence-branch-house-arrest.html https://www.pravda.com.ua/eng/news/2022/03/11/7330474/
This one is actually pretty significant because it's quite possible it could happen, and *actually* be accidental. Russia recently started attacks on western Ukraine; over the weekend, at least one missile strike landed less than 15 miles from the Polish border. https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/russia-ukraine-latest-news-2022-03-11/card/u-s-warns-full-force-of-nato-would-respond-if-russia-hits-poland-zEmQR8bzJCRx5QiKilww https://fortune.com/2022/03/13/nato-will-act-russia-hits-alliance-us/
Word is that China is mostly declining. They won't even sell them aircraft parts. https://www.ft.com/content/30850470-8c8c-4b53-aa39-01497064a7b7 https://www.cnn.com/2022/03/13/politics/jake-sullivan-meeting-chinese-counterpart-ukraine/index.html https://www.nytimes.com/2022/03/13/us/politics/russia-china-ukraine.html
They are installing puppet governors in their stead. https://finance.yahoo.com/news/russian-spy-chiefs-under-house-163301889.html https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/news/putin-purges-russian-intelligence-as-military-elite-suffer-devastating-losses https://www.nytimes.com/2022/03/12/world/europe/ukraine-mayor-kidnapped-ivan-fyodorov.html https://www.rferl.org/a/ukraine-mayor-abducted-russia-dniprorudne/31750806.html https://americanmilitarynews.com/2022/03/putin-arrests-russias-own-spy-chiefs-report-says/
Belarus isn't buying it.https://www.pravda.com.ua/news/2022/03/11/7330425/
Russia has started setting off charges at the Zaorizha nuclear power plant (largest in Europe, provides a huge amount of energy for the continent). We've been hearing for over a week how they may use false flag tactics to blame Ukraine for developing dirty bombs and/or chemical weapons, potentially to justify "small-scale" nuclear attacks against Ukraine (admittedly though, the second half is speculation). This one is just breaking in the last couple of hours, so not much detail yet. Don't know the extent of damages. https://www.ukrinform.net/rubric-ato/3429430-russians-blow-up-part-of-munition-stocks-at-zaporizhia-npp-energoatom.htmlhttps://www.pravda.com.ua/eng/news/2022/03/14/7331247/ https://www.anews.com.tr/world/2022/03/14/ukraine-reports-explosion-near-zaporizhzhia-nuclear-power-plant
item.dc:creator: VikingWeightlifting

item.title: Happy π-day!
item.description: It took me a few seconds to realize what they were doing.
item.dc:creator: IDontCareAtAllButIShould
